  "findings": [
    "Orange high-voltage cable handling violations observed during rough-in stage.",
    "Improper bending radius and physical stress on high-voltage cabling.",
    "Lack of compliance with OEM high-voltage routing and protection specifications."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work on the affected high-voltage circuit immediately.",
    "Inspect the orange high-voltage cables for insulation damage, shielding degradation, or internal conductor stress.",
    "Reroute and reinstall the high-voltage cabling to meet OEM minimum bend radius and securing specifications."
  ],
  "rationale": "High-voltage (HV) orange cables carry lethal voltages in automotive powertrain systems. Violating handling procedures, such as exceeding bend radii or causing physical strain, can compromise the insulation and shielding, creating severe shock, arc flash, and thermal hazards. The work fails compliance and poses a critical safety risk.",
  "references": [
    "OEM HV Assembly and Routing Procedures",
    "NFPA 70E (Standard for Electrical Safety in the Workplace)"
  ]
